assertions.title: No-text / scanned PDF shows user-friendly error
assertions.reason: Uploading an image-only PDF produced the scanned-document guidance message and kept the app on a usable drop screen.

steps.expected: User-friendly scanned/no-text message and no crash
steps.observed: Message containing 'scanned images' displayed; drop screen remained functional
No additional page errors reported by agent-browser errors

assertions.title: Rapid repeated file drops do not break state
assertions.reason: A quick mixed sequence (invalid then valid) resulted in the latest valid PDF being processed to preview with responsive UI and no corrupted state.

steps.action: Upload edge-invalid.txt then immediately upload edge-valid-b.pdf
steps.expected: Latest drop handled; app remains responsive
steps.observed: App transitioned to preview for the valid PDF; action bar and viewer loaded normally
No additional page errors reported by agent-browser errors

assertions.title: Memory pressure — DPI fallback on canvas failure
assertions.reason: The required fallback warning+successful reduced-resolution output behavior was not reliably achieved in-browser.

steps.action: Upload oversized fixtures and trigger Download redacted PDF
steps.expected: 300 DPI render fails, retries at 240 DPI, warning shown, output produced
steps.observed: For edge-large-page-wide-4000x1000.pdf, output completed but no reduced-quality warning appeared; for larger fixtures, flow returned to drop screen with runtime error messages
Console captured jsPDF runtime failures (e.g., 'RangeError: Invalid string length') during failing oversized runs

evidence.consoleErrors: Error in function Array.join (: Invalid string length RangeError: Invalid string length ... at finalizeDoc ...

assertions.issues: Fallback warning text ('Reduced output quality...') was not observed on the done screen in successful oversized output run; larger stress cases produced runtime errors instead of graceful fallback completion.

assertions.title: Graceful handling of corrupted PDF
assertions.reason: Uploading a renamed-JPEG-as-.pdf showed a user-facing validation error and left the app on a usable drop screen without stack trace leakage.

steps.expected: User-friendly error and usable drop screen
steps.observed: Error text 'Invalid PDF structure.' shown on drop screen; UI remained interactive
No additional page errors reported by agent-browser errors

frictions.description: The upload control is hidden (`input[type=file]`), so automation required temporarily exposing it via in-page script before using upload commands.
frictions.resolution: Set inline style on input to make it interactable, then uploaded fixtures.

summary: Tested 4 edge assertions: 3 passed (VAL-EDGE-001, VAL-EDGE-002, VAL-EDGE-004), 1 failed (VAL-EDGE-003) due missing fallback-warning behavior and instability under larger stress fixtures.
